set.body: nightly test-benchmark-regression-worker.R:246 fails with status=error on GPU-less GH macOS runner: in run_group() (tools/benchmark-regression.R:1313) the worker subprocess gets a non-null MLX backend whose available() returns TRUE, runs the matmul, and it throws -> safe_case records error. Session-level amatrix_backend_status(mlx)$available is FALSE on the same host. Worker appears to use the lenient contained-child probe where the session uses the strict direct probe. Contract: worker should degrade to status=unavailable (verified correct locally when backend is NULL). Fix direction: reconcile worker probe with session probe (likely propagate AMATRIX_MLX_PROBE_GPU semantics into the worker env). Diagnostics: nightly snapshot step now if: always(); next red run will carry error_message + backend status. A skip guard would be the wrong fix.
set.title: MLX benchmark worker availability-probe inconsistency: worker runs matmul on runner where session probe says unavailable
